stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of tire pressure using a pressure gauge. The measurements have a mean of 110.1 psi and a standard deviation of 12.0 psi. However, calibration tests reveal that a pressure gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.9% and 7.3%. Estimate the true tire pressure and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 110.1 psi. After correcting for the 5.1% bias: ≈ 104.8 psi. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.9%-7.3%) and sampling variability: [97.8, 111.8]. Point estimate: 104.8 psi.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
